target: Word Order Grammar Game - Punctuation Token Fix
summary: BACKEND: 10/10 tests PASSED. The normalize function fix correctly handles punctuation tokens (comma, period, question mark) stored as separate words. API endpoint /api/unified/lessons/stage_2_unit_07_lesson_01/activity/micro_game_grammar returns word_order items with punctuation as separate tokens (e.g., ['Yes', ',', 'I', 'do', '.']). FRONTEND: Code review confirms fix is correctly implemented in BOTH WordOrder.js (line 64) and UnifiedLessonPage.js (line 1368). UI testing limited due to authentication requirement for unified lesson pages.

WORD_ORDER_PUNCTUATION_FIX.description: Normalize function removes spaces before punctuation marks
test_cases_verified: 'Do you like chicken ?' matches 'Do you like chicken?'
'I like rice and beans .' matches 'I like rice and beans.'
Standard sentences without separate punctuation tokens still work

API_WORD_ORDER_DATA.evidence: Returns word_order game with items containing punctuation as separate tokens: ['Yes', ',', 'I', 'do', '.'] with correctSentence: 'Yes, I do.'

WORD_ORDER_COMPONENT.handleCheck_function: Line 63-73 - uses normalize function to compare user answer with correct sentence

key_tests: test_normalize_function_logic - Tests all punctuation spacing scenarios
test_question_mark_token - Verifies question mark as separate token

tests: Landing page loads without errors - PASSED

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 69 verified the Word Order grammar game punctuation fix. The normalize function in WordOrder.js and UnifiedLessonPage.js correctly removes spaces before punctuation marks when comparing user answers. Backend API tests confirm the data structure has punctuation as separate tokens. To fully test the UI, valid authentication credentials are needed to access /unified/lesson/stage_2_unit_07_lesson_01.

mocked_api.note: All APIs use real MongoDB queries

bug_fix_summary.original_issue: Word Order game incorrectly shows 'Not quite!' when user arranges words correctly because punctuation marks (commas, periods, question marks) stored as separate tokens create extra spaces when joined (e.g., 'Yes , I do .' instead of 'Yes, I do.')
bug_fix_summary.root_cause: The normalize function did not remove spaces before punctuation marks
bug_fix_summary.fix_applied: Added .replace(/\s+([.!?,;:'""])/g, '$1') to normalize function in both WordOrder.js and UnifiedLessonPage.js
bug_fix_summary.verification_method: Backend pytest tests + Code review (UI testing limited by auth requirement)
